domain: Stomatopod saddle bilayer spring — biomineralized bilayer slowly elastically loaded by muscle, then rapidly released to deliver 23 m/s strike via the dactyl club.
specific_mechanism: Slow quasi-static loading of a discrete spring structure separated from the actuator; rapid release of stored elastic energy decoupled from input rate.
llm_application: SADDLE-PRE-WARM: training method introducing a SPRING parameter group with bilayer slow-load + fast-release structure. Slow-load layer accumulates gradient information over K micro-steps (quasi-static preload); on threshold, the fast-release layer applies a high-amplitude update to main weights in a single optimizer step, then both layers reset. Repeated cyclically. Different from gradient accumulation (which is just buffering with no separate group), from WSD (which is LR-schedule), and from momentum (which is per-parameter exponential average).
novelty_claim: Gradient accumulation is a buffer with no parameter group. WSD is an LR-schedule. Momentum is exponential average over single parameter group. SADDLE-PRE-WARM's distinctive contribution is a SEPARATE PARAMETER GROUP with bilayer slow-load + fast-release structure with cyclic preload→release→reset operating outside the main weights.
